# Handoff — rule-cli sync-targets: incremental work + normalized path output

This session diagnosed two `rule sync-targets` defects reported by the user (slow/redundant work; mixed Windows/Unix path separators in output), verified the root causes in source, and recorded a full implementation plan as one spec plus a tracker and three implementation tickets in the `memory-api` ticket/spec store. No production code was changed yet — the next session executes the tickets in order. (Note: the prior handoff about audit-cli rule-overlap docs was set aside; the user's active request is the sync-targets defects.)

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend
- `<MEM-WS>` = ticket/spec workspace selector: `c:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api`
- `SPEC` = spec `9c7c0655-436c-4cd8-a3d3-2d893f1d865c`
- `TRACKER` = ticket `6e7f9bd6-80e8-4f0d-86d9-128eb257eb32`
- `T-PATH` = ticket `2dc02b9b-7e94-4cb4-82c4-83e4359792cb`
- `T-SYNC` = ticket `69b38924-485a-41fb-bdc8-1423b5d82cc2`
- `T-SCAN` = ticket `45f5f58e-4a6e-40b9-bf03-1bc9dc5ca43d`

## Ticket Legend
- `TRACKER` → `6e7f9bd6-80e8-4f0d-86d9-128eb257eb32` — "[rule-cli] sync-targets: incremental work + normalized path output"
- `T-PATH` → `2dc02b9b-7e94-4cb4-82c4-83e4359792cb` — "[rule-cli] Normalize path separators in sync-targets output"
- `T-SYNC` → `69b38924-485a-41fb-bdc8-1423b5d82cc2` — "[rule-cli] sync-targets: skip unchanged writes, single collection pass, reuse SpecStore"
- `T-SCAN` → `45f5f58e-4a6e-40b9-bf03-1bc9dc5ca43d` — "[memory-api] Incremental entity-store scan (mtime/hash short-circuit)"

## Long-Horizon Goal
`rule sync-targets` runs fast by doing work only for rules/targets that actually changed, and emits every path field with forward-slash separators on all hosts, without altering generated file content or the `--check` drift gate. Tracked by `TRACKER`.

## Findings (root causes, verified in source)
- Mixed separators: `generated[].output` and top-level `config` are emitted as raw `PathBuf` (OS separators; backslashes on Windows), while `removed[].output` is forward-slash-normalized via `stable_output_key` → mixed within one payload. See [./memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/rendering.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/rendering.rs) (`"output": output`, `stable_output_key`) and [./memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/dispatch_secondary.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/dispatch_secondary.rs) (`"config"`, `generate_target`/`explain_target` `"output"`).
- Non-incremental store scan: `scan_once` walks every entity, re-reads+parses every manifest, and `integrate_entry` inserts every entity into the metadata index unconditionally — no mtime/hash guard. See [./memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/storage/entity_store.rs](./memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/storage/entity_store.rs) and [./memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/storage/entity_fs.rs](./memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/storage/entity_fs.rs).
- Double rule collection: `ensure_no_zero_match_targets` collects all targets, then `generate_target_payload` re-collects the same rules per target. See `sync_targets_payload` in [./memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/rendering.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/rendering.rs).
- Unconditional writes + per-target SpecStore reopen: `write_generated_output` always writes even when byte-identical; each spec-doc target reopens+rescans a fresh `SpecStore` via `open_spec_store_for_artifact`. Same file as above.
- MCP mirror: [./memory-api/tools/mcp/rule-mcp/src/server/generate.rs](./memory-api/tools/mcp/rule-mcp/src/server/generate.rs) has parallel `write_generated_output`/`generate_target_payload` to keep consistent.

## Decisions Made This Session
- Split work into three independent implementation tickets under one tracker; recorded the contract (6 acceptance criteria) in `SPEC`.
- `T-PATH` is fully independent and lowest-risk; do it first.
- `T-SCAN` is highest-risk because the entity store is shared across rule/ticket/spec/log/audit stores; design the fingerprint guard carefully and add a regression test counting index writes across two no-change scans.

## Key Entities
- [SPEC — sync-targets contract](./memory-api/.spec/specs/9c7c0655-436c-4cd8-a3d3-2d893f1d865c) — acceptance criteria AC1–AC6 and ticket traceability section; the source of truth for "done".
- [TRACKER ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6e7f9bd6-80e8-4f0d-86d9-128eb257eb32/ticket.toml) — parent; `depends_on` all three children.
- [T-PATH ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/2dc02b9b-7e94-4cb4-82c4-83e4359792cb/ticket.toml) — path normalization (AC1).
- [T-SYNC ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/69b38924-485a-41fb-bdc8-1423b5d82cc2/ticket.toml) — skip-unchanged writes, single collection pass, SpecStore reuse (AC2, AC3, AC4, AC6).
- [T-SCAN ticket](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/45f5f58e-4a6e-40b9-bf03-1bc9dc5ca43d/ticket.toml) — incremental entity-store scan (AC5).

## Next Actions (execute in order)
1. `T-PATH`: add a `display_path(&Path) -> String` helper (reuse the `to_string_lossy().replace('\\', "/")` pattern already in `stable_output_key`) and route every emitted path field (rule-cli `sync-targets`/`generate-target`/`explain-target` and rule-mcp `generate`) through it. Add a unit test asserting no `\` in emitted path fields. Move `T-PATH` new → in-implementation → in-review before close.
2. `T-SYNC`: collect target rules once per target and feed both zero-match validation and rendering; in `write_generated_output` skip `fs::write` when `prepared` byte-equals existing and surface a per-target `changed: bool`; open one `SpecStore` per sync run and reuse across spec-doc targets. Keep `--check` drift behavior correct.
3. `T-SCAN`: design a per-entity fingerprint (manifest mtime and/or content hash) in the index sidecar; skip `integrate_entry` for unchanged entities on non-reindex scans; preserve forced-reindex and prune-deleted correctness; add the two-consecutive-scan regression test.
4. After each ticket: run its validation; then re-run `rule sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml` and a `rule sync-targets --check` no-op run; capture before/after timing as efficiency evidence in `SPEC`.
5. Update `SPEC` traceability with validation results; close children then `TRACKER`.

## First Concrete Action
Open [./memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/rendering.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/rendering.rs), add the `display_path` helper, and replace `"output": output` (and the `config` field in [./memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/dispatch_secondary.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/rule-cli/src/cli/dispatch_secondary.rs)) with normalized values.

## First Validation Check
`cargo test -p rule-cli` passes, including a new test asserting emitted `sync-targets` path fields contain no `\` separator.

## Completion Status
- `T-PATH` (AC1): not-started — normalization helper not written yet.
- `T-SYNC` (AC2/AC3/AC4/AC6): not-started.
- `T-SCAN` (AC5): not-started — design step required before coding.
- Validation/timing evidence: not-started — capture after each ticket.
- `SPEC`/`TRACKER` closure: not-started — pending children.

## Definition Of Done
- All `sync-targets` output path fields use `/` on all hosts; `generate-target` and MCP `generate` consistent (AC1).
- Unchanged targets are not rewritten; payload reports `changed` per target (AC2).
- Target rules collected at most once per target per run (AC3).
- One `SpecStore` opened per sync run (AC4).
- Re-running sync with no changed rule files does no per-entity re-integration for unchanged entities (AC5).
- `--check` and the pre-commit drift gate still pass on unchanged inputs and fail on drift (AC6).
- `SPEC` traceability updated with validation results and before/after timing; children and `TRACKER` closed.

## Board / Session Notes
- No board check-in was made this session (planning only). Before implementing, check in on `TRACKER` and claim the files listed in Findings so no overlap with other agents.
- Prior board state showed one unrelated active entry (copilot-interop on log-api files) — no conflict with the rule-cli/entity-store paths above.
- Ticket/spec workspace selector for all commands: `<MEM-WS>`.

## Instruction To Next Session
Execute the Next Actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked. If blocked, record the concrete blocker, evidence, and the smallest viable follow-up slice on the relevant ticket and in `SPEC`.

I'll collect all rules upfront into a map keyed by target, then pass that map to both validation and rendering steps. For skipping unchanged writes, I need to compare the prepared output against what's already on disk and only write if they differ, returning a changed flag. For spec-doc targets, I can use the existing `generated_artifact_matches` function to determine if anything changed before writing. The SpecStore should be opened once at the start of the sync run and reused across all targets rather than being reopened for each artifact.
